1: Sing, O barren, thou that that didst not bear; break forth into singing, and cry aloud, thou that that didst not travail with child: for more are are the children of the desolate than the children of the married wife, saith the LORD LORD .
2: Enlarge the place of thy tent, and let them stretch forth the curtains of thine habitations: spare not, lengthen thy cords, and strengthen thy stakes;
3: for thou shalt break forth on the right hand and on the left; and thy seed shall inherit the Gentiles, and make the desolate cities to be inhabited.
4: Fear not; for thou shalt not be ashamed: neither be thou confounded; for thou shalt not be put to shame: for thou shalt forget the shame of thy youth, and shalt not remember the reproach of thy widowhood any more.
5: For thy Maker is is thine husband; the LORD LORD of hosts is is his name; and thy Redeemer the Holy One of Israel; The God of the whole earth shall he be called.
6: For the LORD LORD hath called thee as a woman forsaken and grieved in spirit, and a wife of youth, when thou wast refused, saith thy God.
7: For a small moment have I forsaken thee; but with great mercies will I gather thee.
8: In a little wrath I hid my face from thee for a moment; but with everlasting kindness will I have mercy on thee, saith the LORD LORD thy Redeemer.
9: For this is as is as the waters of Noah unto me: for as as I have sworn that the waters of Noah should no more go over the earth; so have I sworn that I would not be wroth with thee, nor rebuke thee.
10: For the mountains shall depart, and the hills be removed; but my kindness shall not depart from thee, neither shall the covenant of my peace be removed, saith the LORD LORD that hath mercy on thee.
11: O thou afflicted, tossed with tempest, and and not comforted, behold, I will lay thy stones with fair colours, and lay thy foundations with sapphires.
12: And I will make thy windows of agates, and thy gates of carbuncles, and all thy borders of pleasant stones.
13: And all thy children shall be shall be taught of the LORD LORD ; and great shall be shall be the peace of thy children.
14: In righteousness shalt thou be established: thou shalt be far from oppression; for thou shalt not fear: and from terror; for it shall not come near thee.
15: Behold, they shall surely gather together, but but not by me: whosoever shall gather together against thee shall fall for thy sake.
16: Behold, I have created the smith that bloweth the coals in the fire, and that bringeth forth an instrument for his work; and I have created the waster to destroy.
17: No weapon that is formed against thee shall prosper; and every tongue that that shall rise against thee in judgment thou shalt condemn. This is is the heritage of the servants of the LORD LORD , and their righteousness is is of me, saith the LORD LORD .
